lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Gamble With Your Friends Steam Download and Price
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Check the base game, soundtrack DLC, and current Steam discounts before you buy. A quick pricing pass prevents duplicate purchases across bundles.

steps.title: Check the Base Game Listing
steps.description: Confirm the current base-game price on the official Steam page first so your team starts from the same purchase baseline.

steps.title: Review the Soundtrack DLC Separately
steps.description: The soundtrack is sold as separate DLC, so decide whether your group needs it before checkout to avoid unnecessary add-ons.

steps.title: Compare Bundle Value Before Buying
steps.description: If you want both game and soundtrack, compare bundle pricing against standalone purchases and include active launch discounts.

steps.title: Check Cross-Game Bundle Contents
steps.description: Some bundles include other TENSTACK titles. Confirm whether your group wants only this game or a wider catalog package.

steps.title: Track Price History for Better Timing
steps.description: Use SteamDB trend data to see recent lows and decide whether to buy now or wait for the next sale cycle.

quickTips: Steam launch discounts can end quickly, so verify dates before sharing buy advice.
Bundle pages often update after launch; refresh before final checkout.
For team play, prioritize everyone owning the base game over optional DLC.
Bookmark both Steam Store and SteamDB pages to track future discount windows.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Gamble With Your Friends Gameplay Overview
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Gamble With Your Friends is a co-op casino crawler where one shared account and one shared debt shape every decision from floor to floor.

cards.name: 1-6 Player Online Co-op
cards.description: Runs support solo or full party play, but the core loop is built around team calls under quota pressure.

cards.name: Shared Bankroll Economy
cards.description: Every bet draws from one account, so reckless all-ins hurt the entire team instead of a single player.

cards.name: 5-Minute Quota Days
cards.description: Each day is short and tense: reach quota before time expires, or the run falls behind immediately.

cards.name: Floor Progression and Endings
cards.description: The run climbs themed floors and branches into multiple outcomes based on risk control and team execution.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Gamble With Your Friends Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Start with a simple team plan and avoid early volatility. Most failed first runs come from uncoordinated betting, not bad luck.

items.name: Lobby Plan Before Queue
items.description: Assign one caller for bankroll updates, one timer watcher, and one item lead before entering the first day.

items.name: Pick One Item Goal
items.description: Do not scatter tickets across random buys. Choose one item path that supports your first two floors.

items.name: Start on Familiar Tables
items.description: Use stable opening tables your group understands so everyone can synchronize bet size and timing.

items.name: Use Small Bet Calibration
items.description: Open with small stakes to test rhythm, then raise only after the team agrees on target bankroll pace.

items.name: Call Every Large Bet Out Loud
items.description: Major wagers should always be announced first to prevent overlapping all-ins from separate players.

items.name: Protect Quota-Ready Bankroll
items.description: Once quota is in range, shift to preservation mode and avoid unnecessary high-volatility tables.

items.name: Use Swing Plays Only on Pressure
items.description: Commit to high-risk recovery lines only when timer pressure is real and backup options are exhausted.

items.name: Debrief Each Day Quickly
items.description: Review which table choices worked, then adjust item timing and risk limits before the next floor.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Gamble With Your Friends Co-op Multiplayer
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Multiplayer is friends-first: lobby setup quality matters as much as in-run decisions. Use consistent host and privacy settings for smoother sessions.

solutions.name: Friends-Only or Invite-Only Lobby
solutions.description: Pick explicit privacy mode before invites so random joins do not interrupt role setup and item planning.

solutions.name: Host on Strongest Connection
solutions.description: The most stable host should create the lobby to reduce desync and reconnection issues during high-pressure rounds.

solutions.name: Invite Through Steam Friends
solutions.description: Use the Steam friend list for fast joining, then verify everyone loads in before the first quota day begins.

solutions.name: Set Voice and Callout Rules
solutions.description: Define short callouts for bet size, timer checkpoints, and emergency swing plays to keep comms clean.

solutions.name: Use Discord for Group Assembly
solutions.description: Official community channels help fill parties quickly when your Steam friend list is short.

solutions.name: Re-host After Settings Changes
solutions.description: If privacy or invite state behaves inconsistently, re-host once to apply settings cleanly.

managementTips: No public matchmaking means teammate quality directly determines run stability.
Keep lobby privacy strict when practicing high-risk strategy routes.
One stable host across sessions improves reconnect consistency.
Use the same callout vocabulary every run to reduce chaos under timer pressure.

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Gamble With Your Friends Quota Strategy
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Quota success comes from pacing, not constant maximum bets. Treat each day as a sequence of controlled bankroll checkpoints.

regions.name: Opening Setup Window
regions.description: Open on low-volatility tables and gather quick confidence wins while confirming your team callout rhythm.

regions.name: Midday Scaling
regions.description: Increase stake size gradually once bankroll trend is positive and every large bet is pre-announced.

regions.name: Quota Lock Decision
regions.description: If quota is near complete, transition into preservation instead of chasing extra risk with no strategic upside.

regions.name: Final-Minute Recovery
regions.description: Use a single coordinated swing play only when quota is still out of reach and safer lines are exhausted.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Gamble With Your Friends Floors Guide
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Each casino floor shifts table pressure and recovery options. Teams that adjust their risk profile by floor survive longer.

creatures.name: Floor 1: Stabilize the Bankroll
creatures.description: Focus on consistent tables and build a cushion before trying multiplier-heavy games.

creatures.name: Floor 2: Mix Control and Upside
creatures.description: Use controlled volatility to stay ahead of quota while testing one higher-variance option at a time.

creatures.name: Floor 3: Protect Momentum
creatures.description: Bankroll swings increase here, so clear communication and smaller synchronized bets become more important.

creatures.name: Floor 4: Commit to Team Discipline
creatures.description: Late-game recoveries are possible, but only when the group commits to one shared plan and avoids split decisions.

creatures.name: Layout Variance
creatures.description: Randomized floor layouts change table access, so keep flexible routes instead of fixed scripts.

creatures.name: Floor-Specific Objectives
creatures.description: Achievement hunting should follow floor readiness; forcing niche objectives too early can ruin quota pacing.

creatures.name: Item Timing by Floor
creatures.description: Spend item resources when the current floor risk justifies it, not immediately after purchase.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Gamble With Your Friends Games of Chance
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: The table roster rewards teams that match game volatility to quota pressure instead of chasing every high multiplier.

items.name: Roulette
items.description: Good for controlled openers when the team needs predictable pacing and clear bet communication.

items.name: Blackjack
items.description: Useful when your group wants tighter risk control and cleaner bankroll tracking.

items.name: Slot Machine
items.description: Bring slots in as a planned spike play, not as a default table for every round.

items.name: Street Craps
items.description: Works best when one caller coordinates stake size and stop conditions before each sequence.

items.name: Duck Race
items.description: Fast outcome changes can rescue or sink a day quickly, so use strict cap rules.

items.name: Keno, Crash, HiLo, Plinko
items.description: Reserve these for late pressure or explicit strategy routes to avoid accidental team wipes.

unlockMilestones: Avoid blind max bets on unfamiliar games

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Gamble With Your Friends Best Tables Strategy
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Table selection should change with bankroll state. Winning teams rotate between safe, medium, and high-volatility options with clear triggers.

sections.name: Safe Baseline Rotation
sections.description: Use Roulette or Blackjack to establish early quota progress and reduce panic decisions.

sections.name: Controlled Upside Rotation
sections.description: Mix in one medium-risk table when bankroll is healthy and the timer is under control.

sections.name: Recovery Rotation
sections.description: Only pivot to high-volatility tables when quota deficit is real and the entire team agrees.

sections.name: Stop-Loss Rotation
sections.description: If two large bets fail in sequence, return to safer tables immediately before trying another spike.

growthMilestones: Enter lock mode before final minute
Use emergency swing only when necessary

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Gamble With Your Friends Items and Tickets
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Tickets are limited leverage. Spend them on item plans that support your current floor goals instead of impulse purchases.

priorities.name: Run-Saving Utility Item
priorities.description: Prioritize one item that can stabilize a bad day when quota pressure spikes unexpectedly.

priorities.name: Bankroll Control Item
priorities.description: Choose tools that reduce downside on large bets so one mistake does not erase the team bankroll.

priorities.name: Table-Specific Combo Item
priorities.description: Buy item combos only when your team is committed to the table pattern they support.

priorities.name: Late-Floor Recovery Item
priorities.description: Keep one option in reserve for late-floor quota deficits instead of spending all tickets early.

priorities.name: Cosmetic Spending
priorities.description: Treat cosmetics as surplus spending after your run-critical item path is fully funded.

priorities.name: Body-Part Trade Experiments
priorities.description: Test high-variance shop choices in dedicated practice runs, not in progression-focused sessions.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Gamble With Your Friends Achievements Guide
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: The 55 Steam achievements are easiest to clear in grouped routes: floor goals, game milestones, ending paths, and economy streaks.

achievements.title: Floor 1 Milestones
achievements.description: Clear early objectives while keeping bankroll stability as top priority.

achievements.title: Floor 2 Milestones
achievements.description: Add medium-risk tables once team communication is consistent.

achievements.title: Floor 3 Milestones
achievements.description: Track timer and stake discipline to avoid late-day collapses.

achievements.title: Floor 4 Milestones
achievements.description: Finish high-pressure objectives with a dedicated shot-caller.

achievements.title: Roulette and Blackjack Goals
achievements.description: Use these during controlled runs to build reliable progress.

achievements.title: Slots and Wheel Goals
achievements.description: Schedule volatility achievements when quota buffer is available.

achievements.title: Crash and Plinko Goals
achievements.description: Attempt risky table objectives in coordinated practice sessions.

achievements.title: Keno and Duck Race Goals
achievements.description: Pair these with clear stop-loss rules to protect momentum.

achievements.title: Shared Bankroll Milestones
achievements.description: Track cumulative team discipline rather than individual wins.

achievements.title: Quota Efficiency Milestones
achievements.description: Hit quota earlier in the day to create room for side objectives.

achievements.title: Item Timing Milestones
achievements.description: Use item activations intentionally instead of on cooldown.

achievements.title: Recovery Route Milestones
achievements.description: Practice one consistent comeback pattern for emergency days.

achievements.title: Safe Route Setup
achievements.description: Preserve bankroll and avoid unnecessary volatility across all floors.

achievements.title: Aggressive Route Setup
achievements.description: Commit to controlled high-risk lines only with full-team agreement.

achievements.title: Secret Path Preparation
achievements.description: Reserve these attempts for runs where baseline quota control is mastered.

achievements.title: Cleanup Runs
achievements.description: Use focused runs to finish leftover achievements after core clears.

achievements.title: Session Checklist
achievements.description: Pick one achievement cluster per session to avoid scattered progress.

achievements.title: Role Assignment
achievements.description: Assign shot-caller, timer watcher, and bankroll tracker each run.

achievements.title: Risk Budget
achievements.description: Set daily maximum high-volatility attempts before entering a floor.

achievements.title: Review Loop
achievements.description: After each run, log what failed and update next-session table priorities.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Gamble With Your Friends Endings Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Ending consistency depends on bankroll discipline and team pacing. Use these route notes to avoid accidental derailments.

faqs.question: How many endings are in Gamble With Your Friends?
faqs.answer: Current run structure supports three endings, each tied to different pacing and decision patterns.

faqs.question: When should we push aggressive ending routes?
faqs.answer: Only push high-risk routes after your team can clear quota consistently on controlled runs.

faqs.question: Can we hunt endings and achievements together?
faqs.answer: Yes, but plan one primary objective per run so quota pressure does not split team decisions.

faqs.question: What ruins ending attempts most often?
faqs.answer: Unannounced large bets and off-plan table swaps are the most common causes of failed route setups.

faqs.question: Should we save items for ending runs?
faqs.answer: Reserve at least one recovery-oriented item for late-floor pivots during ending-focused sessions.

faqs.question: How do we practice for consistent ending clears?
faqs.answer: Run repeated floor scripts with fixed callouts, then increase volatility only after baseline clears are stable.

faqs.question: Is solo play useful for ending prep?
faqs.answer: Solo can help learn table behavior, but ending consistency is best tested in full co-op coordination.

faqs.question: Do floor routes matter for endings?
faqs.answer: Yes, floor pacing determines bankroll health and directly affects which ending lines remain practical.

faqs.question: Should we force secret-route attempts every session?
faqs.answer: No, schedule dedicated sessions for secret-route attempts after standard clears become repeatable.

faqs.question: What is the cleanest ending workflow?
faqs.answer: Set a route target in lobby, assign roles, lock a table rotation, and debrief immediately after each attempt.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Gamble With Your Friends System Requirements
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Use these PC requirement checkpoints before launch so your group avoids avoidable setup delays.

faqs.question: What operating system is required?
faqs.answer: Windows 10 or newer is listed for current Steam play sessions.

faqs.question: What processor is listed for minimum setup?
faqs.answer: Core i5-6600 class CPUs are listed for baseline performance.

faqs.question: How much memory is recommended?
faqs.answer: 8 GB RAM is the standard minimum target for stable gameplay sessions.

faqs.question: What graphics baseline should players expect?
faqs.answer: A GTX 1050-level GPU is commonly referenced for the minimum graphics tier.

faqs.question: How much storage should we reserve?
faqs.answer: Keep at least 5 GB free for game files, updates, and local cache growth.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Gamble With Your Friends Troubleshooting and Updates
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: When sessions fail, fix order matters. Run this short checklist before escalating issues to community support channels.

steps.title: Verify Game Update State
steps.description: Apply the latest Steam patch first and restart the client before deeper troubleshooting.

steps.title: Confirm Host and Lobby Settings
steps.description: Re-check lobby privacy and host stability, then re-host if players fail to join consistently.

steps.title: Validate Network Consistency
steps.description: Avoid unstable connection routing and keep all party members on predictable network conditions.

steps.title: Collect Error Context
steps.description: Capture clear repro steps, timestamp, and device details so support replies can target the real failure path.

steps.title: Escalate Through Official Channels
steps.description: Submit the issue in official community spaces with logs and reproducible steps for faster triage.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about this co-op casino crawler.

questions.question: Is Gamble With Your Friends multiplayer only?
questions.answer: No. Solo is available, but the core experience is designed around 1-6 player online co-op coordination.

questions.question: How does the shared bankroll system work?
questions.answer: All players spend from one account, so every large bet affects the entire run and requires team-level risk control.

questions.question: What is the daily quota loop?
questions.answer: Each day gives a short timer to clear quota and keep the run alive, which drives the pacing of every table decision.

questions.question: How many endings and achievements are available?
questions.answer: Current launch content includes three endings and 55 Steam achievements.

questions.question: Where should our team start when learning tables?
questions.answer: Start with lower-volatility tables, lock communication roles, and add high-risk options only when quota pacing is stable.

questions.question: Where can we track updates and community help?
questions.answer: Use the Steam Community Hub and the official Discord for patch notes, troubleshooting, and group coordination.
